channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Pathogenesis & Basic Science
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Pathogenesis & Basic Science

item.title: Oral administration of polyelectrolyte modified curcumin-loaded egg protein nanoparticles for suppression of inflammation in colitis mice.
item.description: International journal of biological macromolecules - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory disease affecting the large intestine, resulting in inflammation to the mucosa and submucosa of the colon. Overproduction of reactive oxygen specie...

item.title: Targeting PKM2-dependent glycolysis reprogrammes monocytes into Cadm1+ macrophages to promote mucosal repair and attenuate colitis progression.
item.description: Gut - Incomplete mucosal healing contributes to progression and relapse in ulcerative colitis (UC). Currently, therapeutic strategies that promote mucosal healing and regeneration are limited and may pot...

item.title: Oxidative stress constrains evolution of bacteriophage host-range diversity.
item.description: The ISME journal - Reactive oxygen species are essential for cellular signalling and redox homeostasis, but their accumulation causes cellular oxidative stress. In inflammatory bowel disease, oxidative stress is link...

item.title: Cross-talk of gut-bone-muscle: osteosarcopenia in experimental colitis models.
item.description: Laboratory animal research - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) represents a group of chronic inflammatory conditions that extend beyond the gastrointestinal tract, with profound systemic effects on the musculoskeletal system. T...

item.title: miR-369-3p Modulates LRRK2-Mediated Inflammation and Autophagy in RAW264.7 Macrophages.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Leucine-rich-repeat kinase 2 (LRRK2) is a multidomain protein highly expressed in immune cells and implicated in the regulation of immune functions including immune signaling, cytokine release and ...
